keyPoints: Custom exceptions are created by extending the Exception class (for checked) or RuntimeException class (for unchecked).
They are thrown using the 'throw' keyword followed by 'new' and the constructor call.
Providing constructors that mirror standard Exception constructors (e.g., a String parameter for a message) is a best practice.
commonMistakes: Extending the wrong base class (e.g., Throwable instead of Exception).
Forgetting to add a throws clause to the method declaration when throwing a checked custom exception.
